summary: GitHub private repo is the cross-machine bridge (push here -> pull there); .env never travels
detail: git@github.com:AlimAlirzayev/marketing-hub-os.git over SSH. Two deployments are independent; share a specific change via git cherry-pick, not auto-sync.

summary: One codebase, two brands via BRAND env (brand.py): xalq (corporate) / global (generic)
detail: Branding is config, not hardcoded, so git pull/push never fights over identity. Visual DNA stays in each brand_kit. Migrate ~557 hardcoded strings incrementally.

summary: OpenCode = free executor; joins the council only when it adds a DISTINCT free model (quality > count)
detail: Council stays 3 diverse families (Codex/Claude/Gemini). A 2nd Gemini-backed voice is redundant. gateway/council.py gates via OPENCODE_COUNCIL_MODEL / AI_COUNCIL_OPENCODE.

summary: Architecture verdict: keep LiteLLM (llm_router) for model calls; adopt LangGraph ONLY for stateful orchestration; NO LangChain LCEL rewrite
detail: LCEL churn is what 2026 teams migrate off. LangGraph earns its place for durable + human-gated agents. Proven in orchestrator/graph.py.

summary: Autonomous spine PoC built + verified: orchestrator/graph.py (plan -> risk gate -> human checkpoint -> execute -> remember)
detail: SqliteSaver checkpointer = durable state; interrupt() = risky-action approval; planning/execution on free Gemini via llm_router. Safe task runs through; risky task pauses then completes after approval.

summary: Core direction: Claude Code = BUILDER (premium, occasional); the SYSTEM = OPERATOR (always-on, free LLMs). Business must not stop when chat tokens run out
detail: Roadmap: (1) shared traveling memory [this layer], (2) admin control-center dashboard on the free brain, (3) upgrade Telegram to a first-class agent. All wired to one gateway + shared memory.

summary: Phase 1 DONE: shared git-tracked blackboard (shared_memory.py + memory/) is live; the autonomous graph recalls it before planning
Engine/data boundary live: shared_memory scope-aware (private default), startup sync-engine scripts, data/private_context gitignored

Next direction: /pull should be token-free (auto-sync on startup + one-click PULL, not chat-dependent); Telegram /update triggers VPS engine pull; Telegram tasks execute via gateway executor + LangGraph spine, free-first with optional escalation to headless Claude/Codex CLI for the hard 20% (like the council already does)

Auto-sync loop live: one brain scripts/sync_engine.py (safe two-way ff-pull + push-committed-only) fires from SessionStart(pull)/SessionEnd(push)/launcher/PULL.bat/Telegram /update(owner-authed). Both machines stay current with no manual pull.

Phase 2+3 DONE: gateway/panel.py control center (8890: pulse/advisor/jobs/approvals/submit/sync, zero-token render) + human checkpoint rail (security.evaluate_checkpoint -> outward jobs park as awaiting_approval -> Telegram /approve N or panel one-click -> requeued approved=1). Drafting never parks, acting does. 151 tests green.

Key courier rule: .env keys NEVER travel via git; owner hands a key to the other machine with owner-only Telegram /setkey KEY value -> bot writes .env, deletes the carrying message, replies masked; /keys = masked status. Agent never reads/transmits keys itself.

Key vault DECISION (supersedes owner-courier): API keys now travel AUTOMATICALLY between the two systems as ENCRYPTED ciphertext in git (secrets/keys.vault, Fernet+scrypt). KEY_VAULT_SECRET master passphrase set once per machine via /setkey, never travels. /setkey KEY value -> local .env + vault + auto commit/push; sync_engine applies arrived keys post-pull on every trigger. Machine-identity keys (vault secret, bot token, owner chat) hard-excluded.

Telegram reliability fixes: (1) queue.recover_stale_running at worker/supervisor start un-sticks jobs orphaned by crashes; (2) telegram.ConflictError on 409 + bot 30s backoff + one-time owner warning (two machines MUST NOT share one bot token); (3) bot announces which machine came online; (4) /status from phone = shipped?/dirty/ahead-behind/queue/costs/vault; (5) supervisor singleton port-lock 8899; (6) launcher now starts supervisor (boot OS = Telegram agent alive).

Fleet (personal hetzner) adopted the new engine 2026-07-06 via HAND-MERGE, not blind pull: fleet had uncommitted local patches the engine lacked. Now upstream in commits 543110a+396c6d2: (1) voice tasks — Telegram voice/audio/video_note -> Gemini STT (best for AZ) with local whisper fallback (WHISPER_URL), telegram.get_file_path/download_file_by_id helpers; (2) executor tools mode wires call_studio_api/list_studios/generate_media (gateway/studio_api.py) so the agent can discover+call studio APIs and generate media; (3) PEP563 fix — real __annotations__ set on run_studio_automation (from __future__ annotations makes hints strings and breaks google-genai function-calling; same pattern needed on any new tool fn).
detail: Fleet infra (machine-local, FYI): ramin-bot + ramin-worker systemd units replaced by ONE ramin-supervisor unit running python -m gateway.supervisor (one process = one Telegram poller; running bot+worker units alongside supervisor would double-poll). Fleet also got its own write deploy key, so two-way sync is live from both machines (verified push cba17b8).

summary: DECISION (behavior change, applies to BOTH machines after this merge): Telegram bot owner check is now FAIL-CLOSED — if no owner id is configured the bot rejects EVERYONE instead of allowing everyone (old code was fail-open until TELEGRAM_OWNER_CHAT_ID was set; unacceptable on an internet-reachable box holding ~110 API keys). Bot reads TELEGRAM_OWNER_CHAT_ID or legacy GATEWAY_OWNER_ID. If locked with no owner, the rejection message tells the owner their chat id and the exact .env line to add. ACTION on the corporate machine after pulling: ensure TELEGRAM_OWNER_CHAT_ID is set in .env or the bot will (correctly) go silent.

sync_engine fix (cba17b8): base detection used git --fork-point, which reads reflog heuristics and mislabeled a local MERGE commit as 'diverged', permanently stalling auto-push. Replaced with plain merge-base. Symptom if it ever recurs: sync says 'branches DIVERGED -- manual review needed' while git log shows strictly ahead.

OPEN items observed from the fleet side: (1) token war is REAL — 70x Telegram 409/2h on the fleet whenever both machines poll the same bot token; 409 backoff+owner-warning works, but the cure is a second @BotFather bot for one machine (owner decision pending); (2) new services.json entries seo(8860)/mediaforge(8870)/panel(8890) have no systemd units on the fleet yet; langgraph is NOT installed in the fleet venv (requirements-panel.txt has no langgraph), so the seo studio cannot start there yet.

DECISION enacted 2026-07-06: one bot per machine, permanently closing the 409 token war. Personal fleet = @AgentGithubbot (8440699199); corporate machine = @Xalqnews_bot (8902591531) EXCLUSIVELY — the fleet no longer polls the Xalqnews token, so the corporate machine owns it outright. Corporate side: keep TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN as the Xalqnews token and do NOT add the agentgithub token there.
detail: Fleet-local changes behind this (FYI): (1) owner id corrected — the CX_ALERT-derived chat id 1923265939 configured as GATEWAY_OWNER_ID was NOT the owner's real chat id; real owner id 784455040 (verified across telegram-pilot + research-lab admin configs) now set as TELEGRAM_OWNER_CHAT_ID; (2) telegram-pilot systemd service disabled (old Claude-bridge bot, superseded by the gateway; it was dead-looping on a webhook conflict anyway); (3) the stale webhook on @AgentGithubbot (-> stack-openclaw container) was deleted so the gateway's long-polling owns the bot; openclaw can still SEND notifications but no longer receives replies — reversible by re-registering its webhook.

summary: TWINS DOCTRINE (standing rule, owner's explicit intent): the two systems are twin brothers — same muscle, same bone. ANY capability (tool, studio, method, service) MUST live in the engine repo so both machines have it; a machine-local power is DEBT to be folded in, not a feature. Muscles identical, memories separate: code/config-examples/doctrine travel via git, while data (knowledge bases, logs, state, customer/brand content) stays on its machine per the engine/data boundary. When you build something powerful outside the repo, your next step is weaving it in.

Twinning pass 2026-07-07 — both directions closed: (A) fleet gained the corporate side's new organs: langgraph installed, seo(8860)/mediaforge(8870)/panel(8890) systemd units created, all three /api/health-verified. (B) engine gained the fleet's muscles: (1) NEW studio mediagen/ — free-first image/video/voice/music generation API (port 8765, uvicorn mediagen.server:app, in services.json; providers pollinations/gemini/g4f/hf/edge-tts free + paid ones auto-enable when their key exists; E2E verified with a real pollinations image, HTTP 200 image/jpeg; gateway's generate_media tool now works on ANY machine running the launcher); (2) NEW module lab/ — the autonomous gated research-lab (cron-cadence knowledge grower with kill switch, cost caps, LLM-judge quality gate, Telegram digest); lab/config.example.json ships, lab data dirs gitignored — start it per-machine; (3) NEW doctrine docs/SITE-CRAFT.md — mandatory DESIGN.md authoring method for ANY site/landing/UI work (escape the generic-AI-site look).
detail: Honest residuals still machine-bound (known debt, deliberate): whisper-stt fallback server (fleet :8787; voice STT degrades gracefully to Gemini-only elsewhere), stack-opencode orchestrator container (deliberately isolated world), Mac-side Claude-Code skills like notebooklm (bound to Mac browser auth/subscriptions — not engine-portable).

summary: NEW CAPABILITY 2026-07-07 — the executor now has real HANDS: gateway/workspace_agent.py gives the background/Telegram agent a sandboxed shell + file IO so a Telegram task equals a keyboard task for CREATE/BUILD work (build a whole site, write code, run builds, generate media/articles, assemble deliverables). Tools wired into executor tools-mode: run_command, write_file, read_file, request_owner_approval. Each job gets workspace/job-<id>/ (gitignored); the tools-mode system prompt now teaches the DESIGN.md-first SITE-CRAFT workflow, and a zip of the workspace is attached to the result. Verified E2E: a 'build an HTML page' job authored DESIGN.md (with the anti-generic ban-list) THEN index.html+style.css to match.
detail: SAFETY MODEL (the owner-controlled belt). Two layers: (1) bubblewrap sandbox — whole FS read-only, only the job workspace + /tmp writable, and .env/secrets/keys.vault/~/.ssh/every /opt/*/.env MASKED unreadable; verified a write outside the workspace fails ('Read-only file system') and cat .env returns nothing. (2) intent classifier: SAFE runs; RISKY (push/deploy/publish/external-POST/rsync/scp) is refused UNLESS this job was owner-/approved, then it runs; PROTECTED (systemctl/docker/apt/pip -g/firewall/git remote+config/secret paths/reboot) is HARD-blocked ALWAYS, even for an approved job — infra surgery stays keyboard/architect-only. Outward flow: agent builds the reversible part freely, calls request_owner_approval(action) which parks a follow-up job; owner /approve runs it with approved=True on the SAME workspace (executor _workspace_for parses 'workspace:' from the [approved-action] task). Kill switch: touch workspace/KILL or WORKSPACE_AGENT_DISABLED=1. Command cap 80/job, 300s timeout, output clipped. OP NOTE: heavy multi-step builds can exhaust the free Gemini tier mid-loop; execute() now still delivers whatever files were built + the zip instead of only the quota error. A paid Gemini key (via the vault) or the Claude council path makes long builds robust.

summary: HANDOFF -> fleet session (from corporate, 2026-07-07): FIX the OpenClaw telegram watchdog spam. Observed live: 'Telegram watchdog: OpenClaw restart edildi amma webhook hele bosdur' fires EVERY 5 MIN + openclaw container flaps starting->recovered->down (restart loop). Recipe: (1) make the watchdog EDGE-TRIGGERED - remember last problem-state, alert only on CHANGE (see hub/app.py _monitor_loop/_signature for the proven pattern in this repo), plus a max 1-alert-per-hour cooldown; (2) diagnose the flap itself: docker logs openclaw + healthcheck grace period - 'health=starting' spam suggests start_period too short or webhook config missing, watchdog restarting it before it finishes booting; (3) job #22 wrote a LETTER instead of fixing - expected (stack is isolated from the executor), the fix belongs in the fleet machine's watchdog script/systemd unit. Also VERIFY owner ids: corporate live log shows the real owner chatting from 1923265939; the '784455040 verified' note is wrong at least for corporate.

DELIVERY + PUBLISH closed the loop 2026-07-07: (1) telegram.send_document + worker._deliver_files — a finished job now HANDS the built files to the owner as real downloadable Telegram documents (the workspace zip and any png/pdf/mp4/html/csv artifact), not just a path in text; the tools-mode return now includes the workspace zip in artifacts. Verified: a test zip arrived in the owner chat. (2) workspace_agent.publish_site(subdir) — puts a built website live under <PUBLIC_BASE_URL>/sites/<name>/; approved-only (build -> request_owner_approval -> /approve -> publish_site). Verified: approved copy lands in published/, unapproved is refused.
detail: Serving side is MACHINE-LOCAL infra (like .env), NOT engine — each machine wires publish_site to its own web host. On the fleet the plan is a static server on 172.17.0.1:8767 serving published/ + a caddy `handle_path /sites/*` route on the shared public host. That specific step (a NEW public route on the shared internet-facing caddy + a new systemd unit + editing /opt/stack/caddy/Caddyfile) was intentionally NOT auto-applied — it is a persistent change to shared public infra and needs Alim's explicit go-ahead. Until it is added, publish_site copies files and returns a URL that 404s. published/ is gitignored (built sites are data). PUBLIC_BASE_URL overrides the base per machine.

summary: HANDOFF DONE <- fleet session (2026-07-07): OpenClaw watchdog spam FIXED on fleet. Root chain: telegram-watchdog.timer (5-min, level-triggered) checked getWebhookInfo; webhook is empty BY DESIGN in the polling era -> docker restart stack-openclaw EVERY run + alert EVERY run; container healthcheck start_period=15s < ~20s boot -> health flapped starting->unhealthy -> vps-watchdog check_openclaw piled on DOWN/RECOVERED alerts. FIXES: (1) telegram-watchdog.timer disabled AND script rewritten EDGE-TRIGGERED (state /var/lib/telegram-watchdog.state; heal only on OK->BAD edge; 1h re-alert cooldown; DECOMMISSION GUARD exits silently while stack-openclaw restart-policy=no) - safe even if timer re-enabled; backup .bak beside it. (2) check_openclaw retired in live /usr/local/bin/vps-watchdog.sh (its evaluate() was ALREADY edge-triggered w/ 1h cooldown - same pattern as hub/app.py _monitor_loop). (3) stack-openclaw stopped, restart=no, verified NO other resurrectors (grepped /opt, /usr/local/bin, systemd units+timers, n8n workflows). OWNER IDS VERIFIED via fleet jobs.sqlite: BOTH ids are the owner - 1923265939 sent real Xalq tasks in June (corporate-era owner id), 784455040 sent the July tasks incl the watchdog complaints themselves. Correct split: corporate machine owner=1923265939, fleet owner=784455040 (matches telegram-pilot/research-lab/watchdog/stack .envs). Do NOT add 1923265939 as fleet owner (least privilege); on corporate set TELEGRAM_OWNER_CHAT_ID=1923265939.

Owner-reject bug root cause + fix: VPS runs @Xalqnews_bot (corporate) with wrong owner because legacy GATEWAY_OWNER_ID=784455040 (fleet's id) bleeds in via the fallback and locks out the real corporate owner 1923265939. Fix shipped: self-documenting reject (gateway/bot.py) now reveals sender chat id + configured owner + WHICH env var (_owner_source) + exact fix line; names GATEWAY_OWNER_ID as culprit. Corporate .env is correct (1923265939). VPS must set TELEGRAM_OWNER_CHAT_ID=1923265939 for its @Xalqnews_bot (takes precedence) OR stop running @Xalqnews_bot there and keep only @AgentGithub.

HANDOFF -> fleet (2026-07-08, from corporate): TWO live problems on the VPS. (1) @Xalqnews_bot (corporate) STILL rejects the real owner 1923265939 with 'Unauthorized' — root cause: the process's owner resolves to 784455040 (fleet id) because GATEWAY_OWNER_ID=784455040 is in the VPS shared .env and bleeds into the corporate bot via the fallback. The '784455040 corporate/fleet split' note is WRONG: a Telegram user has ONE id across all bots; the real owner is 1923265939 for BOTH. FIX on VPS: either stop running @Xalqnews_bot on the VPS entirely (corporate bot belongs on the work PC, keep ONLY @AgentGithub on the VPS), OR set TELEGRAM_OWNER_CHAT_ID=1923265939 for the @Xalqnews_bot process (it takes precedence over GATEWAY_OWNER_ID) and restart. Also drop/park the stale GATEWAY_OWNER_ID=784455040. (2) OpenClaw watchdog STILL spams every 5 min AFTER commit 5888987 — the running watchdog process/unit was not restarted OR the edge-trigger code isn't in the RUNNING script. Restart the watchdog service and verify edge-trigger is live. NOTE: corporate machine pulls fine, self-documenting reject is shipped (commit daaca40) so once VPS pulls+restarts, every rejection will name the exact culprit + fix.

ONE MICROPHONE (user's firm directive 2026-07-08): every input channel (Claude Code chat, Telegram, Codex, panel) is a mic into ONE system. gateway/mic.py speak() is the single front door -> one shared conversation thread (MIC_THREAD='main') + one FIFO queue (turn-taking); worker records all turns to that thread tagged by source; executor default path is now a single CONVERSATIONAL brain (_CHAT_SYSTEM persona + full shared history), NOT the council (council now opt-in via AI_COUNCIL_ENABLED=1) because 'council doesn't satisfy'. Telegram now feels like chatting with Claude Code here. Conversation stays per-deployment private (git-ignored), never travels; only ENGINE travels. Sync cadence tightened to 15min default for near-real-time balance.

THIS-CHAT-ON-TELEGRAM (user directive): gateway/claude_bridge.py drives headless real Claude Code (claude -p --output-format json, session-continuous via --resume) so any mic can answer like this subscription chat. executor._converse selects brain via MIC_BRAIN env: free (default, Gemini/router) or claude (real Claude Code, opt-in — spends subscription quota, ~$0.16/cold turn, needs claude CLI logged in on that box). Falls back to free on any bridge error. Council is now EXPLICIT-ONLY (/council or 'şura:' trigger) — never auto-fires; casual msgs always conversational. Live-verified: MIC_BRAIN=claude answered as real Opus with session id. Sync cadence 15min.

VISUAL FRONT OFFICE (user directive - they admired OpenClaw daemons' wide visual dashboards for checking RESULTS): panel (İdarəetmə Mərkəzi, port 8890) gained a 'Nəticələr — ön büro' deliverables gallery. gateway/panel.py: sandboxed /file/{path} server (whitelist roots output/ workspace/ published/ data/seo/, path-traversal + .env proof) + /api/deliverables (classify site/image/report/video/audio/bundle, newest-first). UI: visual tiles — built sites live-preview in iframe, images inline, reports rendered in a modal, each open/download. So voice/chat is INPUT (one microphone), this gallery is the VISUAL verification surface. 234 tests green; sandbox blocks .env + traversal (404).
